text: Is coding/programming/computering/beepbooping/funking of corn a hobby or a job to you? Or both? It's both for me and I'm increasingly having the two diverge. "Work" coding and "home" coding feel like two entirely different worlds.

text: This one time I went to a tech meetup but it turned out to be a recruiting event in disguise - but I had the "fun" coding hat on and discovered that not that many people wearing "work" hats are interested in PowerBooks and coding Cocoa apps for Mac OS X Tiger.

text:
<@U089PPWPXT9> check this out for gardening, IMO it’s what we have been doing here for years, they just found a nice label for it :), in my Zettelkasten I call it “Begriffsgenese“

<https://blog.muni.town/chatty-community-gardens/|https://blog.muni.town/chatty-community-gardens/>

attachments.text: In the virtual world we think of digital gardens first and foremost as individual, personalized spaces. Yet in our physical cities the pro-social concept of Community Gardens is much beloved and far more accessible to the majority of city dwellers.
attachments.title: Chatty Community Gardens

text:
<https://worksonmymachine.substack.com/p/llm-powered-method-resolution-with|LLM-powered Method Resolution with Synonllm> - I've been experimenting a lot with what new things LLMs have to offer more dynamic languages like Ruby. I'm really hooked on this idea of creating a DSL without an explicit interface, and letting users just call methods semantically and letting the LLM figure it out.

You can see kind of what I mean near the end of the video with the FileHandler class example. In that class the method names are much too long for anyone to really want to type, but that extra detail (combined with the arguments) allows the user to use the interface they'd like and have it just work. Also effectively creating method overloading / dynamic argument-based dispatch in Ruby with the help of an LLM...

attachments.text: Taking Ruby's Principle of Least Surprise to the extreme
attachments.title: LLM-powered Method Resolution with Synonllm

elements.text: LLM-powered Method Resolution with Synonllm
